latest.title: Result of AGM
latest.content:
11 Dec 2025 17:15
RNS Number : 2737L
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
11 December 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED
Registered in Guernsey - Number 60552
LEI NUMBER 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
Registered Office:
1 Royal Plaza, Royal Avenue,
St Peter Port, Guernsey, GY1 2HL
11 December 2025
ANNUAL GENERAL MEETING VOTING RESULTS
The Board of Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited (the "
Company
") is pleased to announce the results of the Annual General Meeting ("
AGM
") which was held at the registered office of the Company at 11.00am on 11 December 2025. Resolutions 1 to 4 were proposed as Ordinary Resolutions and Resolution 5 was proposed as a Special Resolution.
The result of the voting was:
Resolution 1: THAT the Directors be and are hereby authorised to re-appoint Grant Thornton as the Company's auditor, and to fix the auditors' remuneration
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 2: THAT Mr Quentin Spicer be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,158,362 (92.33%)
Votes against: 7,069,259
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 3: THAT Dr Richard Berman be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 4: THAT Mr Anthony Pickford be re-elected as a Director of the Company
Votes for: 85,167,374 (92.34%)
Votes against: 7,060,247
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed
Resolution 5: THAT the Company is approved to continue in existence and the continued implementation by the Board of the Investment Policy and Strategy be approved.
Votes for: 92,227,621 (100.00%)
Votes against: NIL
Votes withheld: NIL
Total Votes Cast: 92,227,621
Result: resolution passed

latest.content:

09 October 2025
ALTERNATIVE LIQUIDITY FUND LIMITED (the "Company")

Share ISIN: GG00BYRGPD65
Share SEDOL:
BYRGPD6
TIDM: ALF
LEI: 213800R5CHD76J3LU713
As at the close of business on 30 June 2025, the final Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.1569
151%
As previously announced, realisation of assets within the Company's portfolio since the end of June, together with the announcement of a distribution to shareholders of $18.99mln being equivalent to $0.13 (13 cents) a share, result in the June 30 NAV being significantly different to the current position. Therefore, in addition to publishing the June 30 NAV, the Board has taken the decision to provide an indicative NAV as at September 30 that incorporates changes to the underlying portlio over the past quarter.
The Board estimates that as at the close of business on 30 September 2025, the indicative Net Asset Value per share of the Company is as follows:
Fund Name
NAV
Quarterly Performance
Alternative Liquidity Fund Limited
USD 0.0365
154% - see Note
Note: the indicative quarterly performance for Sep 30 comprises the distribution to shareholders plus a slight uplift in the underlying portfolio value.
An analysis of the portfolio as at June 30 and September 30 2025 is provided in the quarterly factsheet which is now available on the Company's website, please see the link below.
https://alternativeliquidityfund.com/fact-sheets-and-fund-updates/2025/
Please note that the fact sheet will contain the detailed portfolio update, the Board's observations and conclusions as indicated in the previous shareholder notification of September 22nd.
The final September NAV will be published in due course.

company_30s.what_they_do: Allanfield Group is a small‑cap UK company listed on AIM that specialises in property investment and development.  The business buys, manages and sells residential and commercial real‑estate assets, generating income through leases and capital appreciation.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The group sits in a sector that routinely delivers steady cash flow and has the potential to unlock value through redevelopment or strategic sales.  Its assets are spread across key UK markets, giving it a foothold in a resilient real‑estate cycle.
company_30s.current_state: Recent filings show the company returning capital to shareholders and updating its portfolio, suggesting a shift toward more liquid holdings or a re‑balancing of risk.  The share price is trading well below the book value of its assets, hinting at a possible undervaluation.

rns_narrative: Capital Management: The firm has received new funds and is returning capital, signalling confidence in its cash‑flow generation and a desire to keep shareholders satisfied.
Governance & Performance: AGM results and half‑year financials highlight a focus on maintaining shareholder value and keeping the board engaged.
Portfolio Adjustments: Recent updates to the asset mix point to a strategic review of holdings, possibly preparing for a sale or redevelopment.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet, no chatter – the market is listening, not talking.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: There are no clear bullish voices in the limited social chatter, which is typical for a stock that has been out of the spotlight.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Bears point to the lack of recent earnings, the need for fresh capital, and the risk that the company may be unable to generate sufficient cash flow from its current assets.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The sentiment has stayed flat; no new positive or negative voices have emerged, leaving the market in a holding pattern.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends data is essentially dead, giving the stock a high surprise potential if a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: A fresh funding round or asset sale announced in the next quarter could inject liquidity and lift the share price.
The upcoming half‑year report may reveal a stronger-than‑expected asset‑valuation, providing a technical support level.
A volume spike on a key support level (around the 200‑day moving average) could confirm a bottom.
Any announcement of a dividend or share buy‑back would act as a catalyst for a quick bounce.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 10‑day average volume that exceeds the 50‑day average, a price breakout above the 200‑day moving average, or a positive earnings surprise.  A sudden change in the net asset value or a new property acquisition would also support a bullish thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: The company appears to be in the early stages of a potential recovery.  The APEX score (tighter spring) suggests a sharp rebound is possible once capitulation has finished and a catalyst arrives.

buy_vs_sell.lean: Neutral – the data are insufficient to confirm a deep crash or a clear catalyst, but the dead trends and capital‑return activity provide a contrarian entry point if a catalyst materialises.
buy_vs_sell.bull_case: If the share climbs to roughly the midpoint of its 52‑week range (about 50 % of the peak), the upside would be roughly 100 % from the current price, matching the historical return of a deep crash bottom.  The asymmetry comes from the low probability of a further drop versus the high potential for a rebound.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Without a clear earnings beat or asset sale, the stock could stay in a low‑volume trough for months.  The worst‑case scenario is a further 20 % decline if the company’s asset base fails to generate cash flow.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 20 % of the current price.  Potential gain: 100 % if the price recovers to the 50 % level.  The reward‑to‑risk ratio is roughly 5:1, favouring a contrarian position if the catalyst appears.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter when price closes above the 200‑day moving average and volume is at least 1.5× the 10‑day average.  Size the position so that a 5 % drop would stop you out.  Place a stop at 5 % below the entry to protect against a false breakout.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see either a volume surge at support or a positive earnings release.  If the share price stays below the 200‑day moving average for 30 trading days, consider staying out.
playbook.if_passing: None identified.  No imminent delisting, bankruptcy or fraud signals are present.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Allanfield Group sits in a quiet market with dead trends, but capital‑return activity and a potential asset‑sale catalyst could trigger a sharp bounce from a deep‑crash bottom, offering a highly asymmetric upside if the price recovers to mid‑range levels.
